title: Have I been cited for anything or busted for weed - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about his legal history, sharing stories of police encounters while sleeping in his van across the US, a $40 court fine for parking in a university lot, and near-misses involving weed during travels. He also gives advice on finding cannabis in new cities and dismisses concerns about undercover cops as outdated.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: finding cannabis dealers in new cities

stances.position: a thing of the past, not something to worry about anymore
not speeding is the main key; he was never pulled over when alone

stances.topic: carrying weed while traveling by van
stances.position: just didn't do it; avoided the risk entirely for most of his van life

stances.topic: finding weed in a new city
stances.position: go to a central park area and approach homeless-looking people; easy everywhere he's been

stances.topic: drug enforcement in the southeast US
stances.position: stricter and more unpredictable than the west; would be more cautious there

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a ticket for sleeping in my van in a university parking lot in Colorado.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The judge fined me $40 for sleeping in my van.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think the campus cops should handle parking violations, not regular cops.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been given warnings by cops multiple times for parking or sleeping in my van.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got busted by cops in Tucson for parking near a cop station, even though I asked permission.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A cop in Seattle told me to move my van because people complained about me parking there.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A cop once told me I could stay parked somewhere for up to 24 hours without it being illegal.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got busted in Dallas by security for parking overnight in a fitness center lot.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’ve never been arrested or fined for weed while traveling in my van.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don’t get pulled over by cops because I don’t speed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn’t smoke weed much while living in my van.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got high twice while living in my van—once at a ski resort and once in Las Vegas.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Cops have never searched me or asked about drugs when they’ve stopped me.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was with a friend who got busted for weed in college, and I think the cop planted it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got pulled over once because a cop claimed I was speeding, but I wasn’t.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I traveled with people who smoked weed in my van, but we never got busted.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I almost got caught smoking weed on the side of a highway in Texas when my van broke down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: To find weed in a new city, look for people who stand around downtown and ask them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Undercover cops aren’t a big concern anymore when looking for weed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A YouTube viewer of mine easily found weed in Boulder by asking homeless-looking people in a park.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I’ve scored weed all over the world, including from an Aboriginal guy in Australia.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In some places, like Kansas, cops are strict and will fine you heavily for weed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If you’re stoned and have a gun, you could get into serious trouble.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: West of the Mississippi, cops generally don’t hassle you for weed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It’s unlikely cops will arrest you just for asking someone for weed in most places.
